title: Surprise Party

jokes.text: No, her birthday is the second weekend in May. / That's Mother's Day, honey. / I don't think so.
jokes.setup: David confidently correcting his father about his mother's birthday
jokes.punchline: David doubles down with 'I don't think so' when told Mother's Day isn't his mom's birthday
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - David's oblivious confidence about being wrong is quintessential to his character

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I don't think so.

jokes.text: Maybe Roland's house. / Listen to the words coming out of your mouth.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting Roland's house for an elegant party
jokes.punchline: David's horrified reaction to the absurdity of that suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay showing the class divide and David's horror at tacky suggestions

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We could have the party at Roland's house. It's very spacious.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, no. Absolutely not. Have you seen Roland's house? It looks like a Pottery Barn exploded in a Cracker Barrel.

jokes.text: Oh, snap, David! Talk to the hand, son, because the ears are no longer working.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to be cool and dismissive
jokes.punchline: Hilariously outdated slang delivered with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Johnny's attempts to be hip while being completely out of touch

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm not trying to be cool. I'm just being myself. And myself happens to be totally rad and fly. In fact, I'm the dopest dad in this whole scene, fo' shizzle.

jokes.text: Cancel your trip, Alexis, because the travel agent is out of the office.
jokes.setup: Johnny continuing his 'cool dad' routine
jokes.punchline: Another painfully outdated reference delivered with confidence
jokes.explanation: Continues the cringe comedy of Johnny's outdated attempts at being cool

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You know, I'm going to level with you. When I was your age, I was the coolest guy around. I had the leather jacket, the slicked-back hair, I was listening to all the latest music on my Walkman...

jokes.text: It's so uncomfortable when he does stuff like that. / No. Absolutely not. I feel so weird about it.
jokes.setup: Johnny's embarrassing behavior
jokes.punchline: The kids' mortified reaction to their father's attempts at being cool
jokes.explanation: Good reaction comedy that sells the embarrassment of Johnny's behavior

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God, he's doing it again.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What is he doing?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: He's trying to be cool. He just called my friend a 'homie.'

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes. And he did finger guns.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh God, not the finger guns.

jokes.text: It's so uncomfortable when he does stuff like that. - No. Absolutely not. I feel so weird about it.
jokes.setup: Kids react to Johnny's slang attempts
jokes.punchline: Genuine secondhand embarrassment from his children
jokes.explanation: Kids' mortification at their father's behavior feels genuine and relatable.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my god, dad, please stop trying to use slang.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's literally painful to watch. You sound ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What? I'm just trying to be cool. That's totally lit, fam.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Please never say that again.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm experiencing actual secondhand embarrassment right now.

jokes.text: Chicago's Best Deep Dish Pizza Tour
jokes.setup: Ted planning a romantic getaway
jokes.punchline: The hilariously mundane and unsexy nature of a pizza tour as romance
jokes.explanation: Captures Ted's earnest but unromantic nature perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I've planned us a romantic getaway.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, really? Where?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: A pizza tour of the Tristate area.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, a what?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: We'll visit the five best pizzerias, stay in a Quality Inn, and I've made a spreadsheet of optimal driving routes.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Ted, that's not romantic, that's a logistics presentation.

jokes.text: But, babe, you're going to a veterinary conference in a Chicago suburb, and I think that's... That's really important for us.
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to sound supportive of Ted's career
jokes.punchline: The way she emphasizes how 'important' this unsexy veterinary conference is 'for us'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis trying to be supportive while clearly not caring

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: This conference is just so important. For us.

jokes.text: I'm sure you're from a really solid home and... / David. / Understand. / I'm gonna slap you in the face.
jokes.setup: David making assumptions about Ted's background
jokes.punchline: Ted's direct threat to slap David for his condescending comment
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and character clash - Ted's directness vs David's pretentious assumptions

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I just assumed, given your... rustic upbringing, that you wouldn't understand the finer points of—

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: David, I will slap you.

jokes.text: David, you and Mom literally ruin my life.
jokes.setup: The whole party planning conflict
jokes.punchline: Alexis's dramatic overreaction with 'literally ruin my life'
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis melodrama - perfectly captures her self-centered worldview

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: If this party isn't perfect, it will literally ruin my life.

jokes.text: Well, tempting, but it's gotta be something fancy and elegant. You know, black tie.
jokes.setup: Roland offering to host at his place
jokes.punchline: Johnny's polite rejection while making it clear Roland's place is neither fancy nor elegant
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Johnny's diplomatic but clear class consciousness

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's very kind of you, Roland, but I think we're going to need something a little more... upscale. You know, something with a bit more... finesse.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: What do you mean? My place is nice!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, well, it's nice in the way that a gas station bathroom is nice. Functional, but not exactly where you want to spend an evening.

jokes.text: Roland's entire confused conversation about understanding the plan
jokes.setup: Johnny explaining a simple deception plan
jokes.punchline: Roland's complete inability to grasp or remember basic details
jokes.explanation: Extended sequence showing Roland's lovable incompetence - sustained character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Okay, so here's what we're going to do. You tell them you're my business associate.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Your business associate. Got it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: My name is Johnny Rose.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Right, right. And what's your name?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Johnny Rose. That's... that's what I just said.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh, for you. What about for me?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Your name is Roland Schitt.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Okay, and what's my business?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You're my associate in my business.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Right, but what kind of business?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Just... just say you work with me. That's all you need to remember.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Got it. I work with... Johnny. And your last name?

jokes.text: There is no fundraiser! / Right. No fundraiser. / It's pretend! / Pretend. It's fun time.
jokes.setup: Johnny's growing frustration
jokes.punchline: Roland's childlike 'It's fun time' response to the concept of pretending
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Roland's confusion - 'fun time' is hilariously childish

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, we need to discuss the business model for the motel. This isn't working.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: It's fun time!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, Roland. Fun time is not a business strategy.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: But it's fun time though.

jokes.text: It's a cake. / It's a torte.
jokes.setup: Stevie dismissing Alexis's request as being about cake
jokes.punchline: Alexis's pretentious correction that it's a torte
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Alexis's pretentious distinctions vs Stevie's directness

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: It's not about the cake.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's a torte.

jokes.text: It's a cake. - It's a torte.
jokes.setup: Stevie dismisses David's request as just for cake
jokes.punchline: David's pretentious correction about the dessert type
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - correcting terminology as if it changes the basic request.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You're not getting a cake.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's not a cake, it's a torte.

jokes.text: And wake up chained to a pole in a trucker's basement? Not today.
jokes.setup: Stevie suggesting hitchhiking
jokes.punchline: Alexis's casually specific and dark rejection
jokes.explanation: Excellent character humor - implies Alexis has had wild experiences while being hilariously specific

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: No, absolutely not. I'm not getting murdered on the side of the road by some random person who definitely has a skin suit collection in their basement.

jokes.text: Okay. Well, when you put it like that, it makes me sound ridiculous, um, which I'm not.
jokes.setup: Stevie points out David just wants cake
jokes.punchline: David's defensive assertion that he's not ridiculous (while being ridiculous)
jokes.explanation: David's lack of self-awareness in the face of obvious truth about his behavior.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not being ridiculous. I have a very specific palate, and I refuse to compromise on cake. There's a difference.

jokes.text: Roland's entire butchered invitation delivery
jokes.setup: Roland trying to deliver the rehearsed invitation
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to remember basic details and constant mistakes
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to his earlier confusion - sustained character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You're invited to... uh... a thing. At my place. Next... Saturday? Or was it Sunday? One of those days.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: It starts at... noon-ish? Maybe evening? Wear something nice. Or casual. Whatever you got.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh, and bring a plus one. Or don't. I can't remember if I said that or not.

jokes.text: I'm here to personally invite the two of you to a annual... Elegant dress-up event that Jocelyn is hosting.
jokes.setup: Roland tries to deliver the rehearsed fundraiser invitation
jokes.punchline: Awkward, obviously scripted delivery with wrong grammar
jokes.explanation: Roland's attempt to sound formal fails with bad grammar and obvious rehearsal.

jokes.text: The... With the mouth... Cleft palates.
jokes.setup: Roland trying to remember the fake charity cause
jokes.punchline: His awkward struggle to remember and tactless delivery
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Roland's tactless handling of a sensitive topic

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: It was for... uh... the thing. You know, the... the sick... the sick children? No wait, the blind... no, no, the deaf. Or was it the... the ones with the legs?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Either way, I wrote a very generous check to whatever it was. Very generous. Five figures.

jokes.text: It's hard to look at those kids and all that, I get it. But you know what? We'll dress them up, too.
jokes.setup: Roland misunderstanding Johnny's polite deflection
jokes.punchline: His horrible suggestion to 'dress up' the disabled children
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that shows Roland's complete lack of social awareness

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, that's very kind of you to offer, but we're really not looking for any additional input at this time.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh, I know what you mean. You want to dress them up a little bit, make them look a little less... disabled?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, that's not— no.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, I've got some clip-on ties at the store.

jokes.text: Well, who says I can't do both?
jokes.setup: Alexis sarcastically suggesting Mutt can't take time off from 'bedding women'
jokes.punchline: Mutt's casual confirmation that he could do both simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mutt response - confident and slightly crude in character

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, you can't just take time off from bedding women.

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: No, I could do both simultaneously.

jokes.text: You had a bug in your hair. - Oh. Ew!
jokes.setup: Mutt reaches toward David's hair
jokes.punchline: David's disgusted reaction to having had a bug in his hair
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy moment with David's typical prissy reaction.

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: You had a bug in your hair.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

jokes.text: I was on the board of some of the most profitable non-profits, from San Francisco to London.
jokes.setup: Moira offers to help with fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Oxymoronic phrase 'profitable non-profits' reveals her mindset
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - the contradiction in terms reveals how she views charity work as status symbol.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Profitable non-profits. That's the dream, isn't it?

jokes.text: Jocelyn, you must stop being intimidated by me. It's unnerving.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn being hesitant around Moira
jokes.punchline: Moira's complete lack of self-awareness about why her intimidation is problematic
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Moira - completely self-absorbed while trying to be helpful

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Why does everyone seem so nervous around me? I'm just trying to be helpful and fabulous.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, you literally told Jocelyn her outfit was 'a cry for help' last week.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I was being constructive! How is that intimidating?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You also said her marriage was 'a noble experiment that's clearly failing.'

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, it is. But that's not about me being intimidating, that's just me being honest.

jokes.text: Oh, fuck!
jokes.setup: Jocelyn realizing she's now involved in planning a fake fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Her honest, panicked reaction
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that sells the mounting chaos

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Are we... are we actually doing this? We're actually planning a fake fundraiser?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Oh my God, no. This is insane. I can't— I have a reputation in this town. What if people find out?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I'm going to jail. That's where this is going. I'm going to jail, and Roland's going to have to explain to the kids why Mommy's in prison.

jokes.text: because I think she's going through something right now. I don't know. Something with her body.
jokes.setup: Alexis explains why Stevie wouldn't drive her
jokes.punchline: Vague, inappropriate speculation about Stevie's personal issues
jokes.explanation: Alexis's clueless, invasive assumptions about Stevie's supposed problems.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Stevie wouldn't drive me because she said something about needing to focus on herself right now, and I was like, okay, but also you literally just sit in a car, so I don't know what that means. Like, is she going through something? Does she have a weird thing with cars? I feel like there's clearly something deeper going on that she's not telling me about.

jokes.text: Something with her body.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining why Stevie wouldn't help
jokes.punchline: Her vague, inappropriate speculation about Stevie's personal issues
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's self-absorbed assumptions

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I asked Stevie to help me, but she said she couldn't because she's going through some stuff right now. And I was like, 'Okay, but what kind of stuff? Like, is it a physical thing? Is it emotional? Is it... situational?' And she just walked away, which I'm pretty sure means it's all three.

jokes.text: My God, she had Hillary shaking last year at the Clinton Foundation dinner.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Moira's controlling nature
jokes.punchline: The absurd claim that Moira intimidated Hillary Clinton
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that's both absurd and believable for Moira's character

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom once intimidated Hillary Clinton at a charity gala.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: That didn't happen.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No, it didn't. But I wanted to see if you would believe me, because that's the kind of thing she would do.

jokes.text: Well, that was the exit to Elmdale. We've gone too far, we've gotta turn back!
jokes.setup: Alexis reporting they passed the 'Exit Elmdale' sign
jokes.punchline: David's panicked realization they missed their destination
jokes.explanation: Standard road trip comedy - functional but not particularly original

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, we passed the 'Exit Elmdale' sign? That was our exit.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God, we've gone too far. We're never going to find it now.

jokes.text: The Doctors Who Uncleft The Mouths.
jokes.setup: Question about what organization the fundraiser is for
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's awkward, made-up charity name
jokes.explanation: Good absurd humor - the awkward specificity makes it funny

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: It's for the... uh... The Society for the Preservation of... Llama Wellness and Ethical Grooming Practices.

jokes.text: Do you people know what the word fundraising means?
jokes.setup: Free tickets mentioned for fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Moira's exasperated question about basic fundraising concept
jokes.explanation: Moira's expertise clashing with the fake fundraiser's obvious problems.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Wait, if they're free, how is that a fundraiser?

jokes.text: David, I'm sorry, but it's coup d'etat time.
jokes.setup: Moira getting frustrated with the planning
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic declaration using French political terminology for a party planning takeover
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - overly dramatic language for mundane situations

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: That's it. I am taking over this party planning. I'm declaring a coup d'état.

jokes.text: No, seriously, I might leave. - Okay, last warning. In three. Two.
jokes.setup: Moira threatens to quit planning
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic countdown like a parent disciplining a child
jokes.explanation: Moira's theatrical tantrum using childish countdown tactics.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: That's it. I'm done. I'm quitting.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, come on.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: No. I'm counting to three, and if this isn't resolved, I'm walking out that door. One...

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Two...

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Two and a half...

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Two and three-quarters...

jokes.text: Okay, well, how do you think I got these glasses from the gas station?
jokes.setup: Johnny's shock at her theft suggestion
jokes.punchline: Alexis revealing she's already stolen something else
jokes.explanation: Great escalation revealing Alexis's casual attitude toward theft

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Alexis, I can't believe you're suggesting we steal from the store.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, I already stole the security camera footage from last week, so...

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You what?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What? I looked really cute in it and I wanted to keep it.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I raised a thief. Go wait in the car!
jokes.setup: Learning Alexis stole sunglasses
jokes.punchline: Johnny's parental crisis and treating adult Alexis like a child
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing Johnny's parental instincts despite their circumstances

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You stole sunglasses? Alexis, you're an adult!

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know, daddy.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Do you? Because you're acting like a child. What's next, are you going to shoplift a toy? Steal candy from a baby?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm sorry.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Sorry doesn't cut it. You need to return those sunglasses, and you need to apologize to the store. And frankly, I'm very disappointed in you.

jokes.text: What! There's no fundraiser?
jokes.setup: The surprise party reveal
jokes.punchline: Moira's genuine shock that there was no actual fundraiser
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the deception plot, perfectly in character for Moira

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Wait, there was no fundraiser? But I already planned my outfit!

jokes.text: Oh, never in the history of surprises has one been so delightfully blindsided.
jokes.setup: Moira's reaction to being surprised
jokes.punchline: Her overly dramatic and verbose way of expressing simple surprise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira dialogue - unnecessarily elaborate language for simple emotions

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh my stars! I have been positively ambushed by the element of astonishment! My cardiovascular system is engaged in a most vigorous palpitation, and I find myself bereft of my customary eloquence. This unexpected turn of events has quite thoroughly dispatched my composure to the netherregions!

jokes.text: Yeah, so I need to apologise for my behaviour earlier. - Mmm-hmm. - I think it's just that this whole friend... - It's that lady time, huh?
jokes.setup: Stevie tries to apologize seriously
jokes.punchline: David's inappropriate assumption about menstruation
jokes.explanation: David's awkward interruption with inappropriate assumption during serious conversation.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: David, I want to apologize. I've been a real jerk to you, and I feel terrible about it.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God, are you getting your period?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: What? No!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Because that would explain so much.

jokes.text: It's that lady time, huh?
jokes.setup: Stevie trying to understand Alexis's earlier behavior
jokes.punchline: Her crude and direct assumption about feminine issues
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Stevie's blunt communication style

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: So what, you're just gonna be weird and short with everyone all day?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm not being weird.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You literally just told your brother his hair looked like a dead animal.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, it does.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Okay, so is this a period thing?

jokes.text: Is that okay? - Uh, I normally only slow dance with strangers, at bars... - Right. - After I've had a few... - Uh-huh. But, um, I can make an exception.
jokes.setup: David asks Stevie to dance
jokes.punchline: Stevie's deadpan description of her usual dance partners
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie - dry humor about her typically sketchy romantic encounters.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Will you dance with me?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: My usual dance partners are a bottle of red wine and my own crippling anxiety.

jokes.text: Uh, I normally only slow dance with strangers, at bars... After I've had a few...
jokes.setup: David asking to dance as friends
jokes.punchline: Stevie's deadpan description of her usual dancing circumstances
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie - deadpan revelation about her social patterns

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I usually dance alone in my room with the lights off, and I pretend that nobody can see me.

jokes.text: Um, did you have to kill a man to get it, or... / I did.
jokes.setup: Alexis joking about Mutt's nice suit
jokes.punchline: Mutt's deadpan confirmation that he did kill someone
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor callback to Mutt's mysterious nature

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wow, you look great in that suit. Did you kill someone?

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Yeah.

jokes.text: I wore this thing to prom, like, 1,000 years ago, but it did bring me some luck that night, so I wore it here tonight.
jokes.setup: Mutt explains wearing old suit
jokes.punchline: Mutt hoping for same 'luck' at this party
jokes.explanation: Mutt's casual implication about getting lucky - fits his laid-back character.

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: I wore this suit to my cousin's wedding and he met his wife that night. So I'm hoping for the same luck at this party.

jokes.text: I wore this thing to prom, like, 1,000 years ago, but it did bring me some luck that night, so I wore it here tonight.
jokes.setup: Explanation of where the suit came from
jokes.punchline: Mutt's implication that he's hoping to get lucky again
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mutt's casual approach to romance

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: I'm hoping to get lucky again.

jokes.text: I love my party, John. But please don't ever do it again.
jokes.setup: Moira enjoying her surprise party
jokes.punchline: Her immediate request to never repeat the experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending line that captures Moira's contradictory nature - grateful but controlling

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This is lovely, truly. But I must insist: never do this again. The shock to my system was genuinely traumatic. I nearly fainted, and my heart rate is still irregular. I simply cannot endure another ambush of this magnitude.

tmdb_overview: Johnny tries to arrange a surprise birthday party for Moira. Things backfire when Moira, unwittingly, takes over the planning of her own party.
insight: Character-driven cringe carries a slower episode through escalating social disasters.
